gate0_raw_evidence_precondition.finding: the Gate-A runner (tools/v3_gate_a.py) passed resp.text to the parser but NEVER persisted it; the sealed report retains only input/output tokens, cost, and terminal_state; each cell work-dir holds only ledger.json. No raw text, stop_reason, content-block structure, or raw hash was captured for any of the 18 cells.

recorded_terminal_states_only.note: recorded fields (not raw); reported as fact, NOT used to infer the root cause
recorded_terminal_states_only.narrow_conservative_reading: 0/9 NEW cells were recorded NEW_TRUNCATED, so the parser did not see an unterminated/truncated body; but NEW_MALFORMED merges no-JSON / prose-around-JSON / invalid-syntax and cannot discriminate them without the raw text.

five_decision_questions.q4_uniform_vs_nine_distinct_failures: UNANSWERABLE_WITHOUT_RAW (all share one coarse label NEW_MALFORMED; the underlying nine responses were not retained)

observability_gap: The Gate-A runner did not persist raw responses even though diagnosing format failures was Gate A's explicit purpose. Any future Gate A2 / third-contract run MUST capture, per cell and byte-for-byte: the raw API response, content-block structure (thinking vs text), the exact text handed to the parser, stop_reason, token counts, and a raw hash.
recommendation: Cannot distinguish MODEL_CONTRACT_NONCOMPLIANCE / PARSER_OVERSTRICTNESS / PROMPT_OR_SCHEMA_DESIGN_DEFECT from the retained data. Do NOT proceed to Gate B. If v3 continues, it requires a fresh Gate A whose runner persists raw responses; only then can the format-failure mechanism be diagnosed and a parser-normalization vs third-contract vs close decision be made on evidence. Truncation is not indicated by the recorded terminal states, but that alone does not localize the cause.
